title: Former Eagles linebacker, 6-year NFL veteran, dies
fullText: Dick Absher Jr., who played for four NFL teams over parts of six NFL seasons, died Feb. 25. He was 81.MORE: NFLPA survey reveals worst NFL team; Steelers respond to ESPN bombshellA native of Washington, D.C., Absher starred at linebacker for the University of Maryland from 1964-66. He led the team in receiving in 1964 and 1965 and earned First Team All-ACC honors in 1966.Absher was chosen in the fifth round of the 1967 NFL draft by the Eagles. He would go on to play for the Washington Redskins, Atlanta Falcons, and New Orleans Saints. Absher retired after the 1972 season with Philadelphia, having played 58 games and recording three interceptions to go with five fumble recoveries.AdvertisementAfter his football career ended, Absher served in the United States Army Reserve for eight years. He later worked as a home builder in Maryland.Absher is survived by five children, five grandchildren, and five sisters.More to come on this story.— Enjoy free coverage of the top news & trending stories on The Big Lead —NFL DRAFT: Iowa football star Gennings Dunker steals show at NFL Combine with viral mulletMLB: Bob Costas makes clear statement on Shohei Ohtani’s MLB GOAT statusMMA: Jon Jones shares major news about UFC White House fight cardENTERTAINMENT: 2026 SAG Actor Awards winners list, ‘Sinners’ & Michael B Jordan win bigAdvertisementSPORTS MEDIA: LSU gymnastics star Kailin Chio surpasses Olympic champ Jordan Chiles VIRAL: Florida Man charged for stealing trading cards with taco seasoning packetsThe post Former Eagles linebacker, 6-year NFL veteran, dies appeared first on The Big Lead.
